claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 16 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05); Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. To help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have a child or children that you're inquiring about for Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I'm inquiring about my two children - Alex, who is 6 years old, and Blake, who is 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex and Blake, I need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with: Do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, there are specific circumstances where you may still be eligible for Child Benefit.

Can you tell me which country you're currently living in, and are you a Crown servant (for example, a diplomat or member of HM Forces posted overseas)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm living in Spain at the moment. And no, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that - I'm not a diplomat or in the military.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Since you're living in Spain and are not a Crown servant, I need to understand your status a bit better.

Do you have settled status or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have settled status or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Based on what you've told me, I need to let you know that the eligibility rules for Child Benefit are quite specific regarding living abroad.

According to the official guidance, you can generally only get Child Benefit if you live in the UK, unless you fall into certain specific categories such as:
- Being a Crown servant (diplomat, HM Forces, etc.)
- Having settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with certain conditions if you have pre-settled status)

Since you're living in Spain, are not a Crown servant, and don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, unfortunately you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake under current UK rules.

Just to confirm - have you recently moved to Spain, or have you been living there for some time? And were you previously receiving Child Benefit in the UK before moving?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I've been living in Spain for a while now. I wasn't receiving Child Benefit in the UK before - that's why I'm asking about it now to see if I could claim it.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Based on all the information you've provided, I now have a clear picture of your situation.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete**

I'm sorry to inform you that you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake.

**For Alex (age 6):**
While Alex meets the age requirement (under 16), you cannot claim Child Benefit because you're living in Spain and don't meet the exceptions for claiming while abroad.

**For Blake (age 16):**
Similarly, even though Blake could potentially qualify if in approved education or training, you cannot claim Child Benefit because of your residency abroad without meeting the required exceptions.

**The key issue:** UK Child Benefit normally requires you to live in the UK. The only ways to claim while living abroad are:
- Being a Crown servant (diplomat, HM Forces member, etc.)
- Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme

Since you don't fall into either category, you're unfortunately not eligible to claim Child Benefit while living in Spain.

If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you move back to the UK), you would be able to apply for Child Benefit at that time.

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old and would normally meet the age requirement for Child Benefit (under 16). However, the claimant is living in Spain and does not meet any of the exceptions that would allow them to claim Child Benefit while living abroad. According to the eligibility rules, you normally must live in the UK to qualify for Child Benefit. The exceptions for living abroad are: (1) being a Crown servant, or (2) having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme with certain conditions met. The claimant is not a Crown servant and does not have settled or pre-settled status. Therefore, Alex does not qualify for Child Benefit due to the claimant's residency situation.

Blake is 16 years old and could potentially qualify for Child Benefit if they are in approved education or training (Child Benefit can continue for children under 20 in approved education or training). However, the claimant is living in Spain and does not meet any of the exceptions that would allow them to claim Child Benefit while living abroad. According to the eligibility rules, you normally must live in the UK to qualify for Child Benefit. The exceptions for living abroad are: (1) being a Crown servant, or (2) having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme with certain conditions met. The claimant is not a Crown servant and does not have settled or pre-settled status. Therefore, Blake does not qualify for Child Benefit due to the claimant's residency situation.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is inquiring about Child Benefit for two children: Alex (age 6) and Blake (age 16). While both children would potentially meet the age requirements for Child Benefit if the family were living in the UK, the fundamental issue is that the claimant currently lives in Spain. According to UK Child Benefit rules, you normally must live in the UK to qualify. The only exceptions for living abroad are being a Crown servant or having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. The claimant does not meet any of these exceptions - they are not a Crown servant and do not have settled or pre-settled status. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible to claim Child Benefit for either child.
